input.prompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,

input.initialPrompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

data.content:
Here's the approach:

### 1. Define Resource Dictionaries for each theme

Create three files — `LightTheme.xaml`, `DarkTheme.xaml`, `HighContrastTheme.xaml`:

```xml
<!-- Resources/Styles/LightTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
                    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#FFFFFF</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">#1A1A1A</Color>
    <Color x:Key="AccentColor">#0078D4</Color>
    <!-- ... more colors, styles -->
</ResourceDictionary>
```

Use identical keys in all three dictionaries.

### 2. Merge a default theme in `App.xaml`

```xml
<Application.Resources>
    <ResourceDictionary>
        <ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
            <ResourceDictionary Source="Resources/Styles/LightTheme.xaml" />
        </ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
    </ResourceDictionary>
</Application.Resources>
```

### 3. Create a theme-switching service

```csharp
public static class ThemeService
{
    public static void ApplyTheme(string themeName)
    {
        var mergedDicts = Application.Current!.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
        mergedDicts.Clear();

        var theme = themeName switch
        {
            "Dark"         => new DarkTheme(),
            "HighContrast" => new HighContrastTheme(),
            _              => new LightTheme(),
        };

        mergedDicts.Add(theme);

        // Persist the choice
        Preferences.Set("AppTheme", themeName);
    }
}
```

### 4. Apply the saved theme on startup (`App.xaml.cs`)

```csharp
public App()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    var saved = Preferences.Get("AppTheme", "Light");
    ThemeService.ApplyTheme(saved);
}
```

### 5. Settings page with a Picker

```xml
<Picker x:Name="ThemePicker"
        Title="Choose Theme"
        SelectedIndexChanged="OnThemeChanged">
    <Picker.ItemsSource>
        <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}">
            <x:String>Light</x:String>
            <x:String>Dark</x:String>
            <x:String>HighContrast</x:String>
        </x:Array>
    </Picker.ItemsSource>
</Picker>
```

```csharp
void OnThemeChanged(object sender, EventArgs e)
{
    if (ThemePicker.SelectedItem is string theme)
        ThemeService.ApplyTheme(theme);
}
```

### Key points

- **Use `DynamicResource`** (not `StaticResource`) in all XAML bindings so values update live when the dictionary swaps:
  ```xml
  <Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryText}" />
  ```
- Keep the same keys across all three dictionaries — the swap is seamless because `DynamicResource` re-resolves automatically.
- `Preferences` (from MAUI Essentials) persists the choice across app restarts.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about how to set up themed styling in a .NET MAUI app with light, dark, and high-contrast themes, switchable from a settings page. This is a general "how-to" question, not a code change request against a specific repo. I'll provide a concise architectural answer.
